_comment: OpenSSF Best Practices self-assessment for aksOps/memoryd. Project is registered as id 13135; refresh these fields when criteria scoring changes.
name: memoryd
description: Clean-room Rust memory daemon for AI coding agents and personal long-term memory. It captures useful session context into SQLite, keeps runtime local-first by default, and defers expensive work to governed background jobs.

evidence.private_vulnerability_reporting: GitHub repo setting (private vulnerability reporting enabled)

audit.branch_protection: main protected with required rust status check
audit.latest_local_verification: cargo fmt/build/clippy/test, cargo-deny, cargo-audit, cargo-cyclonedx, shellcheck, gitleaks

description_good_justification: README.md describes memoryd as a local-first Rust memory daemon for AI coding agents and personal long-term memory. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/README.md
interact_justification: README.md documents clone/build/test commands, GitHub issues for feedback, pull requests for contributions, and SECURITY.md for vulnerabilities. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/README.md
contribution_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md explains the pull request process. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
contribution_requirements_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md documents required checks, coding standards, and test policy. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
documentation_interface_justification: CLI and REST interfaces are documented in docs/API.md. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/docs/API.md
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: CI runs on every pull request and push to main. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
license_location_justification: Top-level LICENSE plus LICENSE-MIT and LICENSE-APACHE. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/LICENSE
floss_license_justification: MIT OR Apache-2.0 are FLOSS licenses.
english_justification: Project documentation, issues, and contribution process are in English.
repo_track_justification: GitHub-hosted Git repository tracks changes, authors, and timestamps.
repo_interim_justification: Development happens through public Git commits and pull requests, not only final release archives.
version_unique_justification: Cargo package versions and immutable Git commit ids uniquely identify builds before public releases.
version_semver_justification: Cargo package version uses Semantic Versioning, currently 0.1.0.
release_notes_vulns_justification: CHANGELOG.md includes a security/vulnerability note; no public runtime vulnerabilities have been fixed in a release yet.
report_process_justification: Bug reports use GitHub Issues. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/issues
report_tracker_justification: GitHub Issues is enabled and public.
report_responses_justification: No eligible 2-12 month issue history yet; project policy is to acknowledge reports.
enhancement_responses_justification: No eligible 2-12 month enhancement history yet; project policy is to respond to enhancement requests.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provide a searchable public archive. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/issues
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: SECURITY.md publishes the vulnerability reporting process. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: GitHub private vulnerability reporting is enabled and SECURITY.md documents private reporting. https://github.com/aksOps/memoryd/security/advisories
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md targets acknowledgment within 14 days; no external vulnerability reports in the last 6 months.
build_common_tools_justification: Cargo is the standard Rust build tool.
build_floss_tools_justification: Rust, Cargo, and project dependencies are FLOSS.
test_justification: Automated Rust test suite is run with cargo test --workspace --locked and in CI.
test_invocation_justification: cargo test --workspace --locked is the standard Rust test invocation.
test_most_justification: Current major features have tests for config validation, SQLite migrations, capture, recall, CLI parsing, HTTP auth, and request validation.
test_policy_justification: CONTRIBUTING.md requires tests for major new functionality and bug fixes.
tests_are_added_justification: Recent capture and recall features include store, CLI, HTTP, auth, validation, and no-provider-call tests.
warnings_justification: CI runs cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --locked -- -D warnings; workspace forbids unsafe code.
warnings_strict_justification: Clippy warnings are treated as errors.
know_secure_design_justification: Project design applies least privilege, fail-closed remote bind auth, local-first defaults, no inline provider calls, bounded request bodies, parameterized SQL, and dependency/security gates.
know_common_errors_justification: The project addresses auth bypass, secret leakage, dependency CVEs, SQL injection, unbounded inputs, and unsafe Rust.
crypto_published_justification: Current code does not implement cryptographic protocols.
crypto_call_justification: Current code does not implement cryptography.
crypto_floss_justification: Current code does not implement cryptography.
crypto_keylength_justification: Current code does not implement cryptographic security mechanisms.
crypto_working_justification: Current code does not use broken cryptographic algorithms.
crypto_weaknesses_justification: Current code does not use weak cryptographic algorithms.
crypto_pfs_justification: Current code does not implement key agreement.
crypto_password_storage_justification: Current code does not store passwords.
crypto_random_justification: Current code does not generate cryptographic keys or nonces.
delivery_mitm_justification: Source is delivered through HTTPS GitHub or SSH Git remotes.
delivery_unsigned_justification: Build and bootstrap scripts do not retrieve hashes over plain HTTP; security tool bootstrap uses HTTPS GitHub releases and pinned checksums.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: CI runs cargo-audit and cargo-deny; unresolved medium-or-higher vulnerabilities are not allowed.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: Critical/high vulnerabilities are release blockers via SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, cargo-audit, and cargo-deny.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: GitHub secret scanning and push protection are enabled; local gitleaks scanning reports no leaks.
static_analysis_justification: CI runs clippy, cargo-audit, and cargo-deny before release.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: cargo-audit and cargo-deny check RustSec advisories and dependency policy.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: CI blocks unresolved clippy warnings and dependency advisories.
static_analysis_often_justification: CI runs on pushes to main and pull requests.
dynamic_analysis_justification: Automated tests dynamically exercise SQLite migrations, capture, recall, HTTP auth, and request validation.
dynamic_analysis_unsafe_justification: Project code is Rust with unsafe_code forbidden.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: Rust tests run with assertions enabled.
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: Confirmed exploitable test/dynamic-analysis findings are fixed before release.
maintained_justification: Active development, CI, dependency automation, and security settings are enabled.
